steps.title: Identify what the opening participle requires
steps.content: The sentence begins with a participial phrase: “Knowing that flying insects keep their backs pointed toward sunlight during the day,” The participle “knowing” demands a subject capable of performing that action.

steps.title: Apply the modifier-subject rule
steps.content: A participial phrase at the start of a sentence must logically modify the subject of the main clause. Choice A makes “the researchers” the subject, allowing them to logically perform the action (“knowing”) described by the participle. Choices B, C, and D place “theory” or “moths” as the subject, creating dangling participles because neither can logically know a scientific fact. That matches choice A — A is correct.
